summary: Introductory call with Rajesh from 6th.ai, referred by Sanket/Bitscale. Rajesh had already started working with another GTM partner introduced by Sanket a couple weeks prior. Call was cut short — Rajesh was at a conference and had a flight to catch. He's open to reconnecting in 3-6 months if his current partner doesn't work out.
wentWell: Good rapport — conversation felt natural and professional
Clear value prop communicated: retainer + commission model, full-service GTM
Offered proof-of-concept project as a low-commitment next step
Handled the rejection gracefully, kept the door open
toImprove: Timing was the issue, not price — Sanket introduced other partners 1-2 weeks earlier, should have moved faster on the referral
Call was rushed (14 min of a 60 min block) — Rajesh was at a conference and leaving. Could have rescheduled for a proper deep-dive instead
Didn't ask enough about Rajesh's specific campaign needs (niche pharma/healthcare B2B, event-driven GTM) before jumping into pricing
Should have led with questions about his pain points rather than describing own services
keyTakeaways: Speed matters: when you get a warm referral, reach out within 24 hours
Rajesh values speed and technical GTM execution — not strategy or copy
His campaigns are niche B2B (healthcare/pharma, Salesforce ecosystem), event-driven
He's flexible and willing to complement partner strengths — but needs core performance marketing skills
Follow up in 3-6 months (June-September 2026) to check if current partner worked out
